# @fluenti/solid

> SolidJS bindings for Fluenti — compile-time i18n with signals-based reactivity.

## Overview

@fluenti/solid provides SolidJS components, hooks, and context for internationalization. It uses SolidJS signals for reactive locale switching. Built on @fluenti/core.

- Package: @fluenti/solid
- Docs: https://fluenti.dev
- Repository: https://github.com/usefluenti/fluenti/tree/main/packages/solid
- Peer dependency: solid-js ^1.8
- License: MIT

## Exports

- createI18n(config): Global singleton (createRoot-wrapped), call once at app startup
- createI18nContext(config): Per-component context backed by Solid signals
- I18nProvider: Component providing i18n context to the tree (also sets global singleton)
- useI18n(): Hook returning I18nContext (resolves global singleton first, then nearest provider)
- Trans: Rich text with JSX component interpolation
- Plural: Locale-aware pluralization
- Select: Categorical value selection
- DateTime: Date formatting
- NumberFormat: Number formatting
- msg: Lazy message descriptor

## Quick Start

```tsx
import { I18nProvider, createI18n, useI18n, Trans, Plural } from '@fluenti/solid'

const i18n = createI18n({
  locale: 'en',
  fallbackLocale: 'en',
  messages: { en, ja },
})

render(() => (
  <I18nProvider i18n={i18n}>
    <App />
  </I18nProvider>
), document.getElementById('root')!)

function Greeting() {
  const { t, d, n, locale, setLocale } = useI18n()
  return <p>{t('Hello, {name}!', { name: 'World' })}</p>
}
```

## useI18n() Return Value (I18nContext)

- locale(): Reactive accessor for current locale
- setLocale(locale): Set active locale (async when splitting enabled)
- t(id, values?): Translate a message (supports string or MessageDescriptor)
- d(value, style?): Format a date
- n(value, style?): Format a number
- format(message, values?): Format an ICU message string directly
- loadMessages(locale, messages): Merge additional messages at runtime
- getLocales(): Return all locale codes with loaded messages
- isLoading: Accessor<boolean> for chunk loading state
- loadedLocales: Accessor<Set<string>> of loaded locales
- preloadLocale(locale): Preload a locale in the background
